objective: Evaluate whether a custom CUDA dynamic warp kernel is a better execution route than VPI dynamic Remap payload rebuild for future per-frame mesh/local-warp work, without changing current EIS quality semantics.

entry_context.project_positioning: Jetson Orin NX heterogeneous video compute and device-side dataflow optimization, with EIS as the representative workload.
entry_context.previous_boundary: VPI dynamic Remap works but per-frame payload rebuild is material: standalone NV12_ER dynamic total is about 2.913 ms/frame and MMAPI dynamic Remap stage avg is about 13.1 ms.
entry_context.current_question: Can a custom CUDA dynamic warp kernel update per-frame transform parameters without paying VPI Remap payload rebuild cost, and is it worth considering before any future mesh/local-warp route?
entry_context.quality_boundary: No EIS quality tuning, no change to resid_r15_s07, and no claim that CUDA warp improves stabilization quality.

frozen_variables: Start with standalone CUDA benchmark before any MMAPI integration
Use VPI dynamic Remap standalone timing as the comparison baseline
Do not revive the rejected MMAPI pitch-pointer tearing path without a new safety verifier

result.decision: Standalone CUDA dynamic warp is much faster than VPI dynamic Remap payload rebuild for this affine diagnostic, but MMAPI CUDA scratch integration is deferred to a separate safety verifier.
result.mmapi_decision: Do not directly open MMAPI CUDA writeback in this loop because prior low-level CUDA/pitch-wrapper paths caused tearing; require a new NvBufSurface/EGLImage/CUDA interop verifier first.
result.claim_boundary: Standalone CUDA operator evidence only. No MMAPI integration, no zero-copy, no full real-time EIS, and no EIS quality improvement claim.

task_queue.title: Contract and control-plane setup
task_queue.objective: Register this CUDA dynamic warp loop and freeze the comparison boundary.
success_criteria: onboard points to this active contract while running
doctor passes
VPI dynamic Remap baseline numbers are named
oral template remains unstaged

task_queue.title: CUDA and MMAPI boundary review
task_queue.objective: Inspect existing CUDA/VPI/MMAPI references enough to avoid repeating the rejected tearing route.
success_criteria: standalone CUDA route is separated from MMAPI scratch integration
previous tearing path is not reused as an accepted path
go/no-go for standalone CUDA implementation is clear

task_queue.title: Standalone CUDA dynamic warp
task_queue.objective: Implement and run a minimal CUDA dynamic affine warp kernel on synthetic BGR/RGBA data.
success_criteria: CUDA code compiles on Jetson
identity output matches input or has explained border differences
dynamic transform output is readable
kernel and total timing are recorded
OpenCV or CPU reference diff is recorded

task_queue.title: CUDA vs VPI dynamic comparison
task_queue.objective: Compare standalone CUDA dynamic warp against VPI dynamic Remap with same resolution, frame count, and transform sequence.
success_criteria: same resolution and frame count are used
CUDA timing and VPI timing are compared
output-difference boundary is explained
decision is based on measured data

task_queue.title: Y-plane CUDA dynamic warp
task_queue.objective: If BGR/RGBA CUDA route is promising, implement a Y-plane dynamic warp closer to NV12 scratch semantics.
success_criteria: Y-plane kernel compiles
identity and dynamic transforms run
timing and CPU/OpenCV diff are recorded

task_queue.title: MMAPI integration decision
task_queue.objective: Decide whether to attempt MMAPI CUDA scratch integration based on standalone results and known tearing risks.
success_criteria: decision cites standalone CUDA/VPI data
NvBufSurface/CUDA writeback risks are named
MMAPI branch is skipped unless a safe verifier exists

task_queue.title: Optional MMAPI CUDA diagnostic
task_queue.objective: Only if P5 is favorable, run a minimal MMAPI CUDA identity/translate/dynamic diagnostic.
success_criteria: identity is clean before non-identity
output readability and black border are checked
tearing or sync failures stop the branch

task_queue.title: Knowledge recovery if blocked
task_queue.objective: Use local knowledge, public/official references, or an internal-AI prompt if CUDA/NvBufSurface interop remains unclear.
success_criteria: blocker is stated plainly
local attempts are listed
internal-AI prompt is produced only if needed

task_queue.title: Evidence closeout
task_queue.objective: Write measured CUDA dynamic warp result, ROI, and claim boundary.
success_criteria: report records measured data only
final benchmark/hardware boundary/evidence map are updated if useful
long-term context records high-signal result
review artifacts are copied to the categorized Windows review tree if generated

task_queue.title: Final validation and commit
task_queue.objective: Run JSON, harness, diff, secret, and artifact checks, then commit coherent source/config/docs changes.
success_criteria: JSON parses
doctor passes
git diff --check passes
large artifacts and secrets remain untracked
oral template remains unstaged
commit message trailer policy is followed

forbidden_claims: MMAPI CUDA path accepted without identity and non-identity visual sanity
stop_reasons: Jetson CUDA compiler/runtime is unavailable and cannot be recovered locally
standalone CUDA is slower than VPI dynamic Remap and no engineering value remains
MMAPI CUDA integration would require reviving a known tearing path without a new verifier
